business.headline: Confused by the new tax law? Here are 6 sections that matter most to youThe Income Tax Act 2025 has introduced several updates, leaving many taxpayers unsure about what to track. But once you focus on the most relevant sections, the picture becomes much clearer.

8th Pay Commission: Why your salary hike won't arrive soonA salary hike under the 8th Pay Commission sounds like good news, but the real benefit may not come immediately. There's a gap between announcement and payment, but how long could that delay be?
Sensex ends 510 points higher, Nifty tops 23,100; IT stocks lead rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex added 509.73 points to close at 74,616.58, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 155.40 points to end at 23,123.65.

Air India CEO Campbell Wilson confirms exit, says 'time is right' to step downThe airline said Wilson had informed Chairman N Chandrasekaran about his plans to step down in 2026 as early as 2024, and has since been preparing the organisation for a smooth transition.

Oracle brings in new CFO with big pay after recent layoffsAccording to Oracle's official filing, Hilary Maxson, the new CFO, will receive an annual base salary of $950,000. In addition, she will be eligible for a performance-based bonus with a target of $2.5 million.

Gold, silver prices to stay volatile. Should you buy or avoid?Gold is expected to trade between Rs 144,000 and Rs 154,000 per 10 gm, while silver could move in the Rs 215,000 to Rs 243,000 per kg range before a breakout or breakdown.

What every Indian must understand before buying a life insurance policyLife Insurance is not merely a financial product; it is a safeguard for the people who depend on you. Understanding its purpose, structure and role in financial planning can make the difference between simply owning a policy and truly protecting your family.

Air India hikes domestic fares. Here's how much extra passengers will have to payAir India fare hike: The increase in fuel surcharges comes amid the West Asia war that has led to an energy crisis pushing oil prices higher.

From oil prices to war zones: Why global risks shape your investments nowAt the centre of the recent turmoil lies the Strait of Hormuz — a narrow maritime corridor that carries nearly 20 million barrels of oil daily, roughly one-fifth of global consumption.

IndiGo, Air India CEO exits: Inside the crisis hitting aviation sectorThe exits of CEOs at Air India and IndiGO are not isolated events. They come as the aviation sector grapples with a mix of domestic setbacks and global shocks, raising questions about whether the industry is entering a reset phase.

A shipping crisis grows as West Asia war lifts marine insurance risksThe West Asia war is rippling through the Strait of Hormuz, where soaring war-risk premiums and shrinking insurance cover are slowing ships and quietly driving the cost of global trade.

RBI policy decision tomorrow: Will your home loan EMI go up?As the RBI gears up to announce its policy tomorrow, borrowers are watching closely. Will this be a relief moment or the start of higher EMIs? Let's find out.

Gold, silver prices turn volatile after brief rally. What investors should knowOn the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold is currently hovering around Rs 1,49,000 to Rs 1,50,000 per 10 grams, while silver is trading near Rs 2,31,000 to Rs 2,33,000 per kg.

Faster withdrawals, less paperwork: How EPFO 3.0 will change your PF accessEPFO 3.0 is essentially a digital upgrade of the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's systems, designed to reduce manual intervention and improve service delivery for millions of subscribers.

Govt doubles states' quota of 5 kg LPG cylinders meant for migrant workersIn an order issued on April 6, the government said the daily quantity of 5 kg free trade LPG (FTL) cylinders available for distribution to migrant labourers will be doubled, based on the average daily number of cylinders supplied on March 2 and 3, 2026.
Sensex, Nifty show resilience despite Trump's Iran threatThe S&P BSE Sensex was down by 232.74 points to​​​​​​​ 73,874.11, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 76.35 points to 22,891.90 as of 10:10 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash today as Iran war tensions rise?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,889.5 at 8:01 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open below Monday's closing level of 22,968.25.

Air India CEO Campbell Wilson resigns, to stay till successor namedAir India faces fresh uncertainty as CEO Campbell Wilson steps down, with leadership gaps widening and past controversies still casting a shadow over the Tata Group's ambitious turnaround plans.

DA hike delay: Is this normal or something to worry about?For many, a DA hike around this time is almost expected. But with no announcement yet, the delay is raising a simple question, i.e., is this normal, or is something different this time?

Nayara to shut Vadinar refinery from April 9; will fuel supply take a hit?The timing of this shutdown is important. Ongoing tensions in the Middle East, including the US-Iran conflict, have already tightened the availability of oil and gas globally. This has put pressure on imports of crude oil, natural gas and LPG into India.

entertainment.headline: I am absolutely heartbroken: Lady Gaga cancels Montreal concert due to illness Lady Gaga has halted her Montreal show, citing health concerns and expressed a heartfelt apology to fans. The singer said that she was heartbroken over not being able to perform at the concert.

Ramayana's VFX overload: Big push for Indian cinema or big risk for Ranbir film? Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana is India's biggest shot yet at global-scale VFX-loaded storytelling. However, it arrives at a moment when several high-profile filmmakers internationally have moved away from excessive reliance on digital tools.

Singer Kailash Kher responds to memes about him with lakeer ka fakeer repartee Singer Kailash Kher has reacted to viral memes around his videos, calling out "lakeer ka fakeer" behaviour. He also shared his take on internet humour and cultural habits.

Kartik Aaryan seeks protection of personality rights against unauthorised use Kartik Aaryan has approached the Bombay High Court to safeguard his digital personality rights. The case is scheduled to be heard on April 9.

Rajpal Yadav reacts to bade bhai Salman Khan's support after viral on-stage remark Rajpal Yadav thanked Salman Khan after the superstar defended him amid controversy over a remark made at an event. The exchange struck an emotional chord online.

Always ready for National Awards, says Aditya Dhar amid Dhurandhar 2 success Aditya Dhar reacted to National Award praise for Dhurandhar: The Revenge, saying he is "always prepared" amid the film's massive success.

Prithviraj wraps Varanasi schedule, earns cheat meal, kicks off release countdown Actor Prithviraj Sukumaran has completed a major shooting schedule of the highly anticipated film, Varanasi, by SS Rajamouli. The actor celebrated with a cheat meal and announced the countdown to film's release.

Janhvi Kapoor says Shikhar Pahariya is her wild, no-rules fun zone Actor Janhvi Kapoor spills the tea on what makes her bond with Shikhar Pahariya stand out. Here's what she shared about their relationship and what's next for the actor.

Paid preview maths decoded: The Dhurandhar 2 model, now followed by Bhooth Bangla Paid previews are fast becoming Bollywood's new opening formula after Dhurandhar 2. With Bhooth Bangla following suit, the strategy is now under the spotlight.

Allu Arjun-Atlee's film: Title reveal on actor's birthday, April 8; 1st poster out The first poster for Allu Arjun and Atlee's upcoming collaboration, AA22xA6, has unveiled a mysterious, otherworldly beast. The official title is set to be revealed on the actor's birthday, April 8.

Kanye West's London show in trouble, offers to meet Jewish community Kanye West has reportedly offered to meet Britain's Jewish community amid backlash over his Wireless Festival performance in London.

Coorgi women are beautiful, I'm married to one: Vijay's moving speech for Rashmika Vijay Deverakonda's speech at the Kodagu reception reflected deep affection for Rashmika Mandanna and her heritage. He talked about his visits to Coorg and his connection with the place.

Rapper Offset shot in Florida; 2 detained as police probe incident Rapper Offset was shot in Florida and is currently stable while under hospital care, with two individuals detained as police investigate the incident. Authorities have assured the public that the situation is secure and there is no threat to safety.
Disha Vakani's father, Gujarati actor Bhim Vakani, dies at 84 TV actor Disha Vakani's father and renowned playwright Bhim Vakani died at 84 after battling paralysis and pneumonia.

Anupam Kher calls comedian Samay Raina outstanding at 28, shares life lessons Actor Anupam Kher shared a heartfelt note after meeting comedian Samay Raina and his parents at his acting school. He praised Samay's humour, humility, and reflected on life lessons around making mistakes and learning from them.

Dhurandhar 2 targets historic overtake in Karnataka, set to topple Baahubali 2 Dhurandhar: The Revenge is closing in on a major box office milestone in Karnataka. The film is set to challenge Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's long-standing record as the top non-Kannada grosser.

Salman Khan's million dollar post for Rajpal Yadav after row over awards show remark Salman Khan came out in strong support of colleague Rajpal Yadav after the comedian faced a remark - seen by many as mocking - at a recent awards show.

Dhurandhar beginning of the end for producers: Telugu writer Ravi talks budget gap Filmmaker and writer BVS Ravi compared Telugu films' budgets with Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar, noting key production differences. He said that Dhurandhar's success marks the beginning of the end for producers.

Matka King trailer: Vijay Varma plays ambitious gambler in 1960s Bombay Vijay Varma's upcoming show, Matka King, brings 1960s Bombay's gambling saga to global audiences. It follows an ambitious cotton trader who turns an elite pastime into a nationwide phenomenon.

general news.headline: A whole civilisation will die tonight: Trump warns Iran as deadline loomsTrump still held his cards close to his chest, saying the attack might be called off at the last moment depending on the situation, and described tonight as "the most important moment in the long and complex history of the world."

Live: JD Vance says US confident of Iran's response before Trump's 8 pm deadlineUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: With no signs of the Middle East war abating, strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, a critical oil export hub handling most of the country's crude, have heightened fears over global energy supply and deepening regional tensions. Israel's military said it carried out a wide wave of attacks across Iran, including a petrochemical site in Shiraz, while blasts in Tehran reportedly killed at least 15 people. An Israeli strike also hit the Yahya Abad railway bridge in Kashan, killing two, after warnings urged civilians to avoid train travel. Meanwhile, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed retaliatory strikes targeting US-linked companies in Saudi Arabia, as cross-border hostilities intensified. Iranian projectiles reportedly struck areas in central Israel, causing damage. The King Fahd Causeway between Saudi Arabia and Bahrain has been shut indefinitely amid security concerns. Iran's ambassador to Pakistan described diplomatic efforts as reaching a "critical" stage. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

US hits 50 military targets on Iran's Kharg Island; Trump deadline hours awayThe US hit 50 military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's strategic oil hub, just hours before a deadline set by US President Donald Trump for Iran to strike a ceasefire deal ends at 8 pm ET on Tuesday (5:30 am IST on Wednesday).

Trump extended Iran deadline from 48 hours to 408 hours: Will he push it again?Trump has extended his deadline to Iran three times so far and delayed attacks on energy sites. His new deadline ends today. Will he unleash hell on Iran or give it more time to reopen the Strait of Hormuz?

Salman Khan's million dollar post for Rajpal Yadav after row over awards show remarkSalman Khan came out in strong support of colleague Rajpal Yadav after the comedian faced a remark - seen by many as mocking - at a recent awards show.

Why have passports become a big election issue in Assam?Passports have taken the centre-stage ahead of the Assembly election in Assam. BJP leader Himanta Biswa Sarma earlier used the passport issue to attack Congress's Gaurav Gogoi. The Congress's counter-strike has seen Assam police ending up at Pawan Khera's doorsteps. This is the larger issue behind the passport battle.

Orgasm shot for brides? Big promises, zero clarityFrom weight-loss jabs to intimate fixes, the business of bridal perfection is getting more invasive. But the concern is that while advertisements are everywhere, the silence after enquiry is deafening. Why is that so?

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

RR vs MI Live Score: Rain delays toss in GuwahatiRajasthan (RR) vs Mumbai (MI) Live Score, IPL 2026: An early burst of rain has delayed the toss for the Rajasthan Royals vs Mumbai Indians clash, with the covers firmly in place. Both teams remain in wait-and-watch mode, anxiously tracking the weather as they hope for a timely clearance that allows the contest to begin without further disruption.

Your victory is our victory: Indian astronauts to Artemis 2 crewIn an exclusive interview with indiatoday.in, Indian astronauts Shubhanshu Shukla and Air Commodore Balakrishnan Nair have sent emotional messages to the Artemis 2 crew after their historic lunar flyby on April 7.

Faith first: How Dhurandhar 2 finds its core in Guru Granth Sahib and Bhagavad GitaIn Dhurandhar 2, Aditya Dhar weaves the wisdom of Guru Gobind Singh and Lord Krishna into a spy's journey, in a way today's audience can relate to it. This makes the revenge tale linger long after the credits roll.

Eat 5-star, do nothing: China's strategy amid US-Iran warWhile missiles criss-cross the Middle East and tensions rise, China is doing something unusual - doing nothing. China has refused to get itself dragged into the Iran conflict. The situation is well encapsulated in the latest cover of The Economist - Never interrupt your enemy when he's making a mistake.

What US wants to achieve by invading Kharg islandWhy are the US and Israel targeting Kharg island? By seizing or crippling Kharg, the US aims to starve Iran of oil revenue, force concessions on its nuclear programme and regional influence. But the gamble risks spiralling oil prices and a wider regional war.

Who do you think you are? Tucker Carlson blasts Trump over f-word Iran postTucker Carlson slammed Donald Trump's expletive-filled Easter post on Iran as "vile," saying only someone who sees himself as God would speak in such a manner.

Video: Iranian musician performs outside power plant after Trump threatens attackThe artist, Ali Ghamsari, played outside the Damavand Power Plant, a site that the US has threatened to attack if Iran refuses to open Strait of Hormuz by the end of Trump's deadline.

Video: Iranians form human chain to shield power plants after Trump threatens attackThe development follows a stark warning by Donald Trump, who threatened to launch attacks on Iranian power plants if Tehran did not reopen the strategically crucial Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours.

India now has a nuclear reactor that makes more fuel than it burns. Here's howIndia's Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam has attained a historic milestone. The 500 MWe reactor, which produces more fuel than it consumes, puts India on course to harness its vast thorium reserves.

Internet shut, curfew in Manipur after deadly bomb attack ratchets up tensionsTwo children were killed in a bomb attack on their home in the sensitive Moirang valley of Manipur. The government ordered mobile internet shutdown in five districts and clamped a curfew in Bishnupur to keep the peace amid fears of unrest.

Ever wondered why you get goosebumps? There is hidden science behind itIn this week's edition of The Daily Why, find out why you can feel a chill at the back of your neck or the tiny hair on your arms stand up when you feel cold or scared or excited.

Shady deal? Trump admin's New York hotel pact with Pakistan raises stinkA US-Pakistan deal to redevelop New York's iconic Roosevelt Hotel has raised eyebrows and been called "unimaginable" by a real estate industry expert. While Pakistan said it aims to maximise the value of the property through the deal, the lack of key details on costs, control, and profit-sharing has got many alarmed.

Tejas back in the skies. Did HAL waste years and crores to deliver a dud?LCA Tejas fleets are set to return to the skies after a two-month pause following a rude landing in February. But with decades of delays, cost overruns and repeated setbacks for the LCA Tejas, has Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) spent years and thousands of crores on a dud?

155 aircraft, abandoned airport: Theories question US rescue op in IranThe massive scale and nature of the operation to rescue two US crew members stranded inside Iran, which involved over 155 aircraft, has raised an armada of theories. Moreover, little clarity on why the US destroyed its own aircraft has only added to the intrigue. Trump's narrative of the sequence of events has left several unanswered questions.

sports.headline: Ex-Arsenal and Juventus midfielder Aaron Ramsey announces retirement Aaron Ramsey has announced his immediate retirement from professional football, bringing an end to a 20-year career that saw him represent top clubs including Arsenal and Juventus. The Welsh midfielder also played a key role for the Wales national football team, notably helping them reach the semi-finals of UEFA Euro 2016 and qualify for the FIFA World Cup 2022.

Tamim replaces Aminul Islam as Bangladesh cricket chief after T20 World Cup row Tamim Iqbal takes charge as the new Bangladesh cricket president, replacing Aminul Islam as the country looks to rebuild following the T20 World Cup fiasco.

Will Sooryavanshi treat Bumrah like any other bowler? Pundits predict Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Jasprit Bumrah are all set to battle it out in a prodigy vs master clash when RR face MI in Guwahati on Tuesday, April 8. Aaron Finch and Abhinav Mukund predict how the battle could unfold between the two stars.

Harry Maguire signs contract extension at Manchester United Former captain Harry Maguire has signed a new one-year contract with Manchester United, with the option to extend for a further year. The 33-year-old central defender had been nearing the end of his current deal, which would expire at the conclusion of the season.

Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich: European Clasico returns, odds tilt German way Bayern Munich arrive in Madrid as the more settled side, while Real Madrid rely on big-game moments. As the European Clasico returns, form slightly favours the German champions heading into a high-stakes Champions League quarter-final clash.

Warner arrested for drink-driving in Sydney. Will it affect Pak league stint? Former Australian opener David Warner was arrested for drink-driving in Sydney on Sunday while on a break from his Pakistan Super League duties. Warner has been leading the Karachi Kings in the 11th edition of the PSL.

Jannik Sinner admits missing Carlos Alcaraz rivalry, hopes for Monte Carlo showdown Monte Carlo Masters: Jannik Sinner said he misses facing Carlos Alcaraz and their rivalry, adding that he enjoys their battles because the Spaniard pushes him to his limits.

IPL sacrifices made me lose my England career: Pietersen opens up on ECB fallout Kevin Pietersen said his dispute with the England and Wales Cricket Board over playing in the Indian Premier League led to the premature end of his international career, calling it a personal sacrifice that later benefited other players. He added that his stance helped pave the way for England cricketers to participate more freely in franchise T20 leagues.

Vaishnavi Adkar crowned India's new tennis No.1 with sensational run in 2026 Vaishnavi Adkar's dream 2026 run has taken her to the top of Indian women's singles, with the 21-year-old climbing from World No. 643 to No.383 to become the country's new No.1. She is currently taking part in the Billie Jean King Cup 2026 in New Delhi.

Dump ODIs, stick to T20s and IPL: Lalit Modi's bold suggestion sparks buzz Lalit Modi has stirred debate by calling for ODI cricket to be scrapped, arguing the format no longer fits the modern game shaped by T20 leagues and shifting fan interest. His comments come even as attention slowly builds towards the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Shreyas Iyer's sister not holding back, trolls KKR after washout in Kolkata: WatchIPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer's sister, Shresta, went viral after cheekily trolling Kolkata Knight Riders with an Instagram reel following the rain-hit washout of their clash against Punjab Kings at Eden Gardens.

Hetmyer's form not a concern for RR: Coach backs batter to rise from slumpIPL 2026: RR have downplayed concerns over Shimron Hetmyer's slow start to IPL 2026, backing the West Indies star to come good soon. Performance coach Siddhartha Lahiri stressed that it is too early to judge, insisting the team remains confident in its middle-order strength.

Tim David reveals RCB drill behind 106m six vs CSK: We compete to hit the roofIPL 2026: Tim David revealed RCB's unique roof-hitting drills behind his 106m six vs CSK. The Aussie powered RCB to a massive total with a stunning late assault.

How Jasprit Bumrah, MI can stop RR teen sensationIPL 2026, RR vs MI: Ex-Australia skipper Aaron Finch believes Jasprit Bumrah should target Vaibhav Sooryavanshi early with fuller deliveries and swing when Mumbai Indians face Rajasthan Royals on Tuesday at the Barsapara Cricket Stadium in Guwahati.

Tim David hits 106m six out of Chinnaswamy: But where did the ball disappear? WatchIPL 2026, RCB vs CSK: Tim David lit up IPL 2026 with a monstrous 106-metre six that landed on the Chinnaswamy roof before flying out of the ground during Royal Challengers Bengaluru's clash against Chennai Super Kings on Sunday.

KKR woes deepen: Pathirana still awaiting IPL clearance from Sri LankaIPL 2026: KKR's bowling woes continue as Matheesha Pathirana awaits IPL clearance. The Sri Lankan pacer could join the squad only by mid-April.

Mumbai Indians play down Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hype, Ravi Bishnoi no mysteryRR vs MI, IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians play down Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's hype ahead of RR clash, with Paras Mhambrey saying there are no special plans, while also dismissing Ravi Bishnoi as a mystery threat.

Green not good enough? Pietersen's scathing take on KKR's misfiring starKevin Pietersen delivered a blunt take on Cameron Green after the Australian all-rounder continued to struggle in the IPL 2026 campaign. Green scored just four runs during the match against PBKS.

Graceful in defeat, Sheetal Devi helps limbless archer Payal Nag on podium: VideoSheetal Devi showed rare grace in defeat, helping limbless archer Payal Nag face the flag on the podium. The teenager from Odisha had just stunned the world number one to win gold in the World Archery Para Series in Bangkok.

Heavenly intervention: Rain washes away KKR's toss blunder vs PBKS in KolkataIPL 2026, KKR vs PBKS: Kolkata Knight Riders were reeling at 25 for 2 in the fourth over when rain intervened, forcing a no-result in Kolkata. Ajinkya Rahane's decision to win the toss and opt to bat under overcast skies drew sharp criticism.

Did R Ashwin take a dig at Rahane's call to bat vs Punjab in rainy Kolkata?R Ashwin was surprised by KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane's call to bat first in rainy Kolkata against PBKS at the Eden Gardens on Monday, April 6. Rahane justified his call to bat first, but not everyone was convinced by the decision.

Where is the hype? Cameron Green flops yet again for KKR, fans left frustratedKKR vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Cameron Green's poor run continues as fans question his role and price, with KKR under pressure after back-to-back losses and missing Narine and Chakravarthy in IPL 2026 clash.

KKR without Sunil Narine, Varun Chakravarthy for first time in 7 yearsKKR vs PBKS, IPL 2026 Playing XIs: Kolkata Knight Riders will not have both Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine in their side for the first time in seven years.

Will MI captain Hardik Pandya be ready for RR clash? Bowling coach offers updateMumbai Indians have offered an update on Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder missed the loss against Delhi Capitals. Hardik missed the game due to illness.

PSL to beat IPL? The financial reality behind Mohsin Naqvi's No. 1 league claimPCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi claims the PSL will soon become the most sought-after T20 league in cricket. However, with the IPL's $18.5 billion valuation dwarfing the PSL's $260 million, the financial reality suggests a massive divide.

Is Avesh Khan in trouble? Pacer's antics after Pant's winning runs go viralAvesh Khan faces scrutiny after a boundary-side incident during LSG's win over SRH, adding to the franchise's growing concerns over contentious decisions this IPL season.

How Test retirement unlocked Kohli as a T20 batter in IPL? R Ashwin explainsRavichandran Ashwin explains how Test retirement helped Virat Kohli improve his T20 strike rate with a new batting approach after the RCB vs CSK clash. Kohli scored 28 off 18 balls and gave RCB early momentum in their innings.

Sanju Samson vs Jasprit Bumrah: India's T20 World Cup heroes in hunt for ICC awardSanju Samson and Jasprit Bumrah lead ICC Player of the Month nominees after starring in India's T20 World Cup triumph, while South Africa's Connor Esterhuizen impressed with a breakout series in New Zealand.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
